title: Inside api-oauth2 — the OIDC / OAuth2 Authorization Server
subtitle: box = component [technology] · arrow = uses / calls · issues tokens; the private signing key stays in backend-auth
callersLabel: Callers · edge + internal (context — each has its own L3)
callers: ext:nginx proxy|[HTTPS] RP apps / browser — direct edge

cards: +Auth Backend Client|[Thrift client]|→ backend-auth: clients · codes · consent · signing key

downstreamLabel: Downstream · containers / datastore (context — each has its own L3)
downstream: ext:backend-auth|[Thrift backend]|clients · codes · JWT signing key
ds:KeyDB|[datastore · Redis]|token / code state · SSO blacklist
rels: /authorize → api-auth (session) → Consent → issues an auth code
/token → Auth Backend Client → backend-auth signs the JWT (private key never leaves backend-auth)
Discovery + JWKS publishes the public keys api-gateway uses to verify
note: api-oauth2 is its own public edge behind the nginx proxy — a peer of api-gateway, not behind it; token / discovery / JWKS endpoints do not pass through api-gateway.
refs: signing key held by backend-auth (L3) · session from api-auth (L3) — appears in Dynamic: OAuth2 token · Login + MFA — zoom out: Container view Auth & Tokens (L2)
caption: Identity Platform · Component view · api-oauth2 (C4 L3) — components from api/ + action/ + client/
